subject: When the card is the hard part
preview: A few words can make the whole gesture feel more like you.

hero.headline: when the card is the hard part
hero.subheadline: A few words can make the whole gesture feel more like you.

opt-out.optOutBody: We understand. If you'd prefer not to receive gifting reminders, you can opt out below.

body-main.headline: The flowers were easy. It's the card that stopped you.
body-main.bodyP1: You picked the bouquet in three minutes. But then you sat there, pen hovering, wondering if 'Happy Birthday' was enough. Or too much. Or somehow both at once.
body-main.bodyP2: Here's the thing: the card isn't an afterthought. It's where the flowers become yours. Where a beautiful gesture becomes a personal one.

steps.headline: Three Approaches That Actually Work
steps.step1Text: Be Specific — Not 'thinking of you' but 'thinking of that time we got lost in the botanic gardens'.
steps.step2Text: Be Honest — 'I'm not great at this, but I wanted you to know I care' says more than any poem.
steps.step3Text: Be Brief — Two sentences, handwritten. That's the whole card.

product-1.productOccasion: For the one who makes every day feel like a celebration

product-2.productOccasion: For when words aren't quite enough on their own

body-closing.headline: The best messages sound like you
body-closing.bodyP1: Not a greeting card. So write like you'd speak — just a little more thoughtful. And if you need a starting point, we've got a whole guide waiting for you.
